subject: PATIENT
heading: Resolved Problems:__page_no__2
text: resolved problems: * no resolved hospital problems. * operations during hospitalization: no none procedures during hospitalization: echocardiogram and heart catheterization: no intervention hospital course: patient is a 85-year-old male with past medical history of coronary disease, hyperlipidemia, a-fib on eliquis(recent diagnosis), hypothyroidism presented to the er with recent worsening shortness of breath and a syncopal episode at home. ct of the chest in the er was positive for bilateral pulmonary emboli. patient also diagnosed with new congestive heart failure. echocardiogram showed lvef of 18% with a large lv thrombus. patient is treated with lasix, heparin drip and started on aspirin statin metoprolol and jardiance. patient during the hospitalization had a sustained monomorphic ventricular tachycardia. he is seen by cardiology and transferred to cvicu. patient is started on amiodarone for ventricular tachycardia. patient underwent a left heart catheterization that showed moderate coronary artery disease no intervention was needed. patient initially was also started on entresto but unable to continue secondary to hypotension. no ace or arb started secondary to hypotension. patient seen by electrophysiologist and recommended discharging home with lifevest. he will need outpatient follow-up in 3 months with repeat echocardiogram for possible aicd placement. patient also noted to elevated creatinine. nephrology is consulted. suspect ckd rather than aki. no previous labs available. renal ultrasound showed trabeculated bladder with calculus in the bladder. he started on finasteride as well as tamsulosin patient also noted to have an incidental finding of t9 compression deformity on mri of the t-spine.

subject: PATIENT
heading: Resolved Problems:__page_no__2
text: resolved problems: * no resolved hospital problems. * operations during hospitalization: no none procedures during hospitalization: echocardiogram and heart catheterization: no intervention hospital course: patient is a 85-year-old male with past medical history of coronary disease, hyperlipidemia, a-fib on eliquis(recent diagnosis), hypothyroidism presented to the er with recent worsening shortness of breath and a syncopal episode at home. ct of the chest in the er was positive for bilateral pulmonary emboli. patient also diagnosed with new congestive heart failure. echocardiogram showed lvef of 18% with a large lv thrombus. patient is treated with lasix, heparin drip and started on aspirin statin metoprolol and jardiance. patient during the hospitalization had a sustained monomorphic ventricular tachycardia. he is seen by cardiology and transferred to cvicu. patient is started on amiodarone for ventricular tachycardia. patient underwent a left heart catheterization that showed moderate coronary artery disease no intervention was needed. patient initially was also started on entresto but unable to continue secondary to hypotension. no ace or arb started secondary to hypotension. patient seen by electrophysiologist and recommended discharging home with lifevest. he will need outpatient follow-up in 3 months with repeat echocardiogram for possible aicd placement. patient also noted to elevated creatinine. nephrology is consulted. suspect ckd rather than aki. no previous labs available. renal ultrasound showed trabeculated bladder with calculus in the bladder. he started on finasteride as well as tamsulosin patient also noted to have an incidental finding of t9 compression deformity on mri of the t-spine.

text: history of present ilness: [patient_name] is a 85-year-old man with past history of cad, newly diagnosed atrial fibrillation on apixaban (started 3 weeks prior to presentation), hypothyroidism, and hyperlipidemia. the patient presents to the hospital on [encounter_date] with shortness of breath which was worse with exertion and dizziness. dyspnea actually started around christmas time in summer 2023. dyspnea has been progressive and more frequent in the last month. the patient saw his pcp in mid january 2024 and was placed on antibiotic. the patient was seen again by his pcp 3 weeks prior to this admission, and the patient was noted to have atrial fibrillation by his pcp. he was started on apixaban at around the same time, 3 weeks prior to presentation. he presented to hospital on [encounter_date] mainly because of syncopal episode which occurred on [encounter_date]. workup in ed revealed multiple subsegmental pulmonary embolisms. ct scan also revealed small bilateral pleural effusion. nephrology is asked to see the patient because of elevated serum creatinine on presentation on [encounter_date] at 1.31 mg/dl. serum creatinine is 1.36 mg/dl today with serum bicarbonate level of 18 mmol/l. serum bicarbonate level was 25 mmol/l on presentation. he denies diarrhea overnight or today. patient denies current chest pain or pressure. dyspnea has improved. he denies lower extremity edema. the patient does have urinary hesitancy and dribbling although these are not new symptoms. he denies chronic use of nsaids. there are no prior serum creatinine available for comparison.

text: history of present ilness: [patient_name] is a 85-year-old man with past history of cad, newly diagnosed atrial fibrillation on apixaban (started 3 weeks prior to presentation), hypothyroidism, and hyperlipidemia. the patient presents to the hospital on [encounter_date] with shortness of breath which was worse with exertion and dizziness. dyspnea actually started around christmas time in summer 2023. dyspnea has been progressive and more frequent in the last month. the patient saw his pcp in mid january 2024 and was placed on antibiotic. the patient was seen again by his pcp 3 weeks prior to this admission, and the patient was noted to have atrial fibrillation by his pcp. he was started on apixaban at around the same time, 3 weeks prior to presentation. he presented to hospital on [encounter_date] mainly because of syncopal episode which occurred on [encounter_date]. workup in ed revealed multiple subsegmental pulmonary embolisms. ct scan also revealed small bilateral pleural effusion. nephrology is asked to see the patient because of elevated serum creatinine on presentation on [encounter_date] at 1.31 mg/dl. serum creatinine is 1.36 mg/dl today with serum bicarbonate level of 18 mmol/l. serum bicarbonate level was 25 mmol/l on presentation. he denies diarrhea overnight or today. patient denies current chest pain or pressure. dyspnea has improved. he denies lower extremity edema. the patient does have urinary hesitancy and dribbling although these are not new symptoms. he denies chronic use of nsaids. there are no prior serum creatinine available for comparison.

heading: Acute kidney injury.__page_no__10
text: acute kidney injury. patient presented with elevated serum creatinine 1.31 mg/dl on [encounter_date]. since there is no prior available serum creatinine for comparison, we will presume that this is acute for now. another possibility that this is chronic elevation of serum creatinine due to ckd. will try to get any older laboratory data from his pcp, [provider_name]. i will check urinalysis and renal ultrasound to assess kidney size and structure particularly since he has lower urinary tract symptoms. further workup will be based on results of these preliminary studies. serum creatinine is about the same today. there is no urgent need for kidney replacement therapy. i will recheck renal function panel later today mainly to reassess acid-base status. if patient is discharged today, i will have him follow-up with me in 1 to 2 weeks. current medications are reviewed and are appropriate dose for his renal function.

subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: Interim History:__page_no__12
text: history of present illness interim history: transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name], transition from inpatient hospitalization, transition to home (own), admission date [encounter_date], 30 day readmission no, number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. 1, discharge date [encounter_date], discharge summary report received yes operations during hospitalization: none procedures during hospitalization: echocardiogram and heart catheterization: no intervention hospital course: patient is a 85-year-old male with past medical history of coronary disease, hyperlipidemia, a-fib on eliquis(recent diagnosis), hypothyroidism presented to the er with recent worsening shortness of breath and a syncopal episode at home. ct of the chest in the er was positive for bilateral pulmonary emboli. patient also diagnosed with new congestive heart failure. echocardiogram showed lvef of 18% with a large lv thrombus. patient is treated with lasix, heparin drip and started on aspirin statin metoprolol and jardiance. patient during the hospitalization had a sustained monomorphic ventricular tachycardia. he is seen by cardiology and transferred to cvicu. patient is started on amiodarone for ventricular tachycardia. patient underwent a left heart catheterization that showed moderate coronary artery disease no intervention was needed. patient initially was also started on entresto but unable to continue secondary to hypotension. no ace or arb started secondary to hypotension. patient seen by electrophysiologist and recommended discharging home with lifevest. he will need outpatient follow-up in 3 months with repeat echocardiogram for possible aicd placement. patient also noted to elevated creatinine. nephrology is consulted.

text: history of present illness interim history: transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name], transition from inpatient hospitalization, transition to home (own), admission date [encounter_date], 30 day readmission no, number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. 1, discharge date [encounter_date], discharge summary report received yes operations during hospitalization: none procedures during hospitalization: echocardiogram and heart catheterization: no intervention hospital course: patient is a 85-year-old male with past medical history of coronary disease, hyperlipidemia, a-fib on eliquis(recent diagnosis), hypothyroidism presented to the er with recent worsening shortness of breath and a syncopal episode at home. ct of the chest in the er was positive for bilateral pulmonary emboli. patient also diagnosed with new congestive heart failure. echocardiogram showed lvef of 18% with a large lv thrombus. patient is treated with lasix, heparin drip and started on aspirin statin metoprolol and jardiance. patient during the hospitalization had a sustained monomorphic ventricular tachycardia. he is seen by cardiology and transferred to cvicu. patient is started on amiodarone for ventricular tachycardia. patient underwent a left heart catheterization that showed moderate coronary artery disease no intervention was needed. patient initially was also started on entresto but unable to continue secondary to hypotension. no ace or arb started secondary to hypotension. patient seen by electrophysiologist and recommended discharging home with lifevest. he will need outpatient follow-up in 3 months with repeat echocardiogram for possible aicd placement. patient also noted to elevated creatinine. nephrology is consulted.

text: history of present illness interim history: transition of care inpatient name of the hospital [facility_name], transition from inpatient hospitalization, transition to home (own), admission date [encounter_date], 30 day readmission no, number of in patient admissions in last 12 months. 1, discharge date [encounter_date], discharge summary report received yes operations during hospitalization: none procedures during hospitalization: echocardiogram and heart catheterization: no intervention hospital course: patient is a 85-year-old male with past medical history of coronary disease, hyperlipidemia, a-fib on eliquis(recent diagnosis), hypothyroidism presented to the er with recent worsening shortness of breath and a syncopal episode at home. ct of the chest in the er was positive for bilateral pulmonary emboli. patient also diagnosed with new congestive heart failure. echocardiogram showed lvef of 18% with a large lv thrombus. patient is treated with lasix, heparin drip and started on aspirin statin metoprolol and jardiance. patient during the hospitalization had a sustained monomorphic ventricular tachycardia. he is seen by cardiology and transferred to cvicu. patient is started on amiodarone for ventricular tachycardia. patient underwent a left heart catheterization that showed moderate coronary artery disease no intervention was needed. patient initially was also started on entresto but unable to continue secondary to hypotension. no ace or arb started secondary to hypotension. patient seen by electrophysiologist and recommended discharging home with lifevest. he will need outpatient follow-up in 3 months with repeat echocardiogram for possible aicd placement. patient also noted to elevated creatinine. nephrology is consulted.

subject: PATIENT
heading: History of Present Illness :: Interim History:__page_no__12
text: suspect ckd rather than aki. no previous labs available. renal ultrasound showed trabeculated bladder with calculus in the bladder. he started on finasteride as well as tamsulosin patient also noted to have an incidental finding of t9 compression deformity on mri of the t-spine. findings reflect chronic lesion from bony fragmentation but underlying pathological lesion not entirely excluded. is recommended an outpatient follow-up with mri with contrast versus pet scan. patient is ordered for repeat bmp to be done in 4 days labs and procedures pending at discharge: no pending results., date discharge summary was received: [encounter_date], final discharge diagnoses received yes reason for hospitalization: syncope principal problem: acute systolic chf (congestive heart failure) active problems: left ventricular apical thrombus pathological fracture of vertebra due to neoplastic disease aki (acute kidney injury) acute pulmonary embolism syncope coronary artery disease involving native coronary artery of native heart without angina pectoris vt (ventricular tachycardia) ischemic cardiomyopathy nicm (nonischemic cardiomyopathy) chronic atrial fibrillation resolved problems: * no resolved hospital problems. *, post-discharge other consultation/followup yes cardiologist, nephrologist, cardiac electrophysiologist, results pending or testing ordered post-discharge none, discharged with home care or ancillary therapy: no, does the patient have a care manager? yes, date care manager notified: [encounter_date], assistance needed with adl's: no, oral nutritional supplements given inpatient or prescribed homegoing no, attempted outreach yes, initial contact date [encounter_date], initial contact within 2 business days of discharge yes, initial

text: suspect ckd rather than aki. no previous labs available. renal ultrasound showed trabeculated bladder with calculus in the bladder. he started on finasteride as well as tamsulosin patient also noted to have an incidental finding of t9 compression deformity on mri of the t-spine. findings reflect chronic lesion from bony fragmentation but underlying pathological lesion not entirely excluded. is recommended an outpatient follow-up with mri with contrast versus pet scan. patient is ordered for repeat bmp to be done in 4 days labs and procedures pending at discharge: no pending results., date discharge summary was received: [encounter_date], final discharge diagnoses received yes reason for hospitalization: syncope principal problem: acute systolic chf (congestive heart failure) active problems: left ventricular apical thrombus pathological fracture of vertebra due to neoplastic disease aki (acute kidney injury) acute pulmonary embolism syncope coronary artery disease involving native coronary artery of native heart without angina pectoris vt (ventricular tachycardia) ischemic cardiomyopathy nicm (nonischemic cardiomyopathy) chronic atrial fibrillation resolved problems: * no resolved hospital problems. *, post-discharge other consultation/followup yes cardiologist, nephrologist, cardiac electrophysiologist, results pending or testing ordered post-discharge none, discharged with home care or ancillary therapy: no, does the patient have a care manager? yes, date care manager notified: [encounter_date], assistance needed with adl's: no, oral nutritional supplements given inpatient or prescribed homegoing no, attempted outreach yes, initial contact date [encounter_date], initial contact within 2 business days of discharge yes, initial

text: progress note: [provider_name] [encounter_date]

subject: PATIENT
heading: No Heading Found__page_no__13
text: office visit, reconciliation of medication completed yes. discharge medication reconciliation: medications reconciled: current and discharge- keep inpatient facility discharge medications. discharge records: record: reviewed by provider. status improving- continues to wear external defibrillator (has not had any symptoms and has not given him any warnings). not having any trouble at home and went out for two one mile walks yesterday. very active and hoping to get back to the gym. has been compliant with his meds. not checking bp but denies feeling lightheaded or dizzy. no sob, cp, or edema. no back pain. tolerating all his new meds well and feels he is able to empty his bladder better than he did before. has an upcoming appt with nephrology in 2 days, ep cardiology in june and cardiology in april. brief review of i/p labs, tsh 5.64, cr 1.62 with gfr 41, albumin decreased (3.4), increased urine protein. cbc nl, bnp1400. doppler for dvt negative. echo wih ef 18%, lv apical thrombus, multiple pe's. ct chest with t9 compression fx with ? lytic lesion. mri thoracic spine with evidence of chronic compression facture of t9 may reflect bony fragmentation but underlying pathologic lesion not entirely excluded. us kidney 4 cm bladder stone, increased pvr. ct brain with chronic transcortical infarct left frontoparietal complex, ? subacute phase infarction and cerebral atrophy. had episode of sustained v-tach and taken emergently to cath lab. diffuse 2 vessel disease and one 80% lesion (di). to continue medical management. congestive heart failure: c/o chf f/u feels good. states that breathing is better, denies any associated palpiations or leg edema (has never been aware of the a-fib). doing daily weights and baseline/discharge weight listed at 140 pounds. admits doesn't have much appetite and wife is struggling with what to feed him between competing cardiac and renal diets. not checking his bp since he got home. c/o shortness of breath improving.

text: office visit, reconciliation of medication completed yes. discharge medication reconciliation: medications reconciled: current and discharge- keep inpatient facility discharge medications. discharge records: record: reviewed by provider. status improving- continues to wear external defibrillator (has not had any symptoms and has not given him any warnings). not having any trouble at home and went out for two one mile walks yesterday. very active and hoping to get back to the gym. has been compliant with his meds. not checking bp but denies feeling lightheaded or dizzy. no sob, cp, or edema. no back pain. tolerating all his new meds well and feels he is able to empty his bladder better than he did before. has an upcoming appt with nephrology in 2 days, ep cardiology in june and cardiology in april. brief review of i/p labs, tsh 5.64, cr 1.62 with gfr 41, albumin decreased (3.4), increased urine protein. cbc nl, bnp1400. doppler for dvt negative. echo wih ef 18%, lv apical thrombus, multiple pe's. ct chest with t9 compression fx with ? lytic lesion. mri thoracic spine with evidence of chronic compression facture of t9 may reflect bony fragmentation but underlying pathologic lesion not entirely excluded. us kidney 4 cm bladder stone, increased pvr. ct brain with chronic transcortical infarct left frontoparietal complex, ? subacute phase infarction and cerebral atrophy. had episode of sustained v-tach and taken emergently to cath lab. diffuse 2 vessel disease and one 80% lesion (di). to continue medical management. congestive heart failure: c/o chf f/u feels good. states that breathing is better, denies any associated palpiations or leg edema (has never been aware of the a-fib). doing daily weights and baseline/discharge weight listed at 140 pounds. admits doesn't have much appetite and wife is struggling with what to feed him between competing cardiac and renal diets. not checking his bp since he got home. c/o shortness of breath improving.

text: office visit, reconciliation of medication completed yes. discharge medication reconciliation: medications reconciled: current and discharge- keep inpatient facility discharge medications. discharge records: record: reviewed by provider. status improving- continues to wear external defibrillator (has not had any symptoms and has not given him any warnings). not having any trouble at home and went out for two one mile walks yesterday. very active and hoping to get back to the gym. has been compliant with his meds. not checking bp but denies feeling lightheaded or dizzy. no sob, cp, or edema. no back pain. tolerating all his new meds well and feels he is able to empty his bladder better than he did before. has an upcoming appt with nephrology in 2 days, ep cardiology in june and cardiology in april. brief review of i/p labs, tsh 5.64, cr 1.62 with gfr 41, albumin decreased (3.4), increased urine protein. cbc nl, bnp1400. doppler for dvt negative. echo wih ef 18%, lv apical thrombus, multiple pe's. ct chest with t9 compression fx with ? lytic lesion. mri thoracic spine with evidence of chronic compression facture of t9 may reflect bony fragmentation but underlying pathologic lesion not entirely excluded. us kidney 4 cm bladder stone, increased pvr. ct brain with chronic transcortical infarct left frontoparietal complex, ? subacute phase infarction and cerebral atrophy. had episode of sustained v-tach and taken emergently to cath lab. diffuse 2 vessel disease and one 80% lesion (di). to continue medical management. congestive heart failure: c/o chf f/u feels good. states that breathing is better, denies any associated palpiations or leg edema (has never been aware of the a-fib). doing daily weights and baseline/discharge weight listed at 140 pounds. admits doesn't have much appetite and wife is struggling with what to feed him between competing cardiac and renal diets. not checking his bp since he got home. c/o shortness of breath improving.

text: office visit, reconciliation of medication completed yes. discharge medication reconciliation: medications reconciled: current and discharge- keep inpatient facility discharge medications. discharge records: record: reviewed by provider. status improving- continues to wear external defibrillator (has not had any symptoms and has not given him any warnings). not having any trouble at home and went out for two one mile walks yesterday. very active and hoping to get back to the gym. has been compliant with his meds. not checking bp but denies feeling lightheaded or dizzy. no sob, cp, or edema. no back pain. tolerating all his new meds well and feels he is able to empty his bladder better than he did before. has an upcoming appt with nephrology in 2 days, ep cardiology in june and cardiology in april. brief review of i/p labs, tsh 5.64, cr 1.62 with gfr 41, albumin decreased (3.4), increased urine protein. cbc nl, bnp1400. doppler for dvt negative. echo wih ef 18%, lv apical thrombus, multiple pe's. ct chest with t9 compression fx with ? lytic lesion. mri thoracic spine with evidence of chronic compression facture of t9 may reflect bony fragmentation but underlying pathologic lesion not entirely excluded. us kidney 4 cm bladder stone, increased pvr. ct brain with chronic transcortical infarct left frontoparietal complex, ? subacute phase infarction and cerebral atrophy. had episode of sustained v-tach and taken emergently to cath lab. diffuse 2 vessel disease and one 80% lesion (di). to continue medical management. congestive heart failure: c/o chf f/u feels good. states that breathing is better, denies any associated palpiations or leg edema (has never been aware of the a-fib). doing daily weights and baseline/discharge weight listed at 140 pounds. admits doesn't have much appetite and wife is struggling with what to feed him between competing cardiac and renal diets. not checking his bp since he got home. c/o shortness of breath improving.

text: office visit, reconciliation of medication completed yes. discharge medication reconciliation: medications reconciled: current and discharge- keep inpatient facility discharge medications. discharge records: record: reviewed by provider. status improving- continues to wear external defibrillator (has not had any symptoms and has not given him any warnings). not having any trouble at home and went out for two one mile walks yesterday. very active and hoping to get back to the gym. has been compliant with his meds. not checking bp but denies feeling lightheaded or dizzy. no sob, cp, or edema. no back pain. tolerating all his new meds well and feels he is able to empty his bladder better than he did before. has an upcoming appt with nephrology in 2 days, ep cardiology in june and cardiology in april. brief review of i/p labs, tsh 5.64, cr 1.62 with gfr 41, albumin decreased (3.4), increased urine protein. cbc nl, bnp1400. doppler for dvt negative. echo wih ef 18%, lv apical thrombus, multiple pe's. ct chest with t9 compression fx with ? lytic lesion. mri thoracic spine with evidence of chronic compression facture of t9 may reflect bony fragmentation but underlying pathologic lesion not entirely excluded. us kidney 4 cm bladder stone, increased pvr. ct brain with chronic transcortical infarct left frontoparietal complex, ? subacute phase infarction and cerebral atrophy. had episode of sustained v-tach and taken emergently to cath lab. diffuse 2 vessel disease and one 80% lesion (di). to continue medical management. congestive heart failure: c/o chf f/u feels good. states that breathing is better, denies any associated palpiations or leg edema (has never been aware of the a-fib). doing daily weights and baseline/discharge weight listed at 140 pounds. admits doesn't have much appetite and wife is struggling with what to feed him between competing cardiac and renal diets. not checking his bp since he got home. c/o shortness of breath improving.

text: 6. cerebral atrophy notes: noted on ct brain in setting of ? chronic infarction. currently does not appear to have any significant cognitive impairment 7. compression fracture of t9 vertebra with routine healing, subsequent encounter notes: discussed ct and mri findings. currently feels well, not having any back pain. declines further work up at this time. understands this could be related to a cancerous process, but feels that he would be unlikely to pursue any extensive work up or treatment of a cancer should he have one. goal of care is to stay as active as he can as long as he can and right now he is able to do that and he feels good
